message:
fix(indexer): skip empty incremental scans in watch mode (#194)

An empty incremental watch scan (conv_count == 0) was falling through
into the full ingest path — committing an empty Tantivy segment,
updating last_indexed_at, and then waking optimize_if_idle. On a live
watch process this produced repeated "Preparing commit" / "save metas"
/ "Running garbage collection" cycles when nothing had actually
changed.

Two narrow changes:

- reindex_paths(): early `continue` when conv_count == 0, after the
  watch_scan log line so the empty-scan event is still recorded.
- watch loop optimize_if_idle() calls (both branches): gate on
  `indexed > 0` so empty scans do not trigger segment merge. The
  300s-cooldown / 4-segment threshold inside optimize_if_idle() only
  suppresses over-merging during activity; it did not stop being
  called.

Extended reindex_paths_does_not_advance_watch_state_when_scan_yields_no_conversations
to assert `t_index.segment_count() == 0` after the empty-fixture
scan. segment_count() == 0 transitively proves neither the commit
nor the last_indexed_at bump happened (they are set back-to-back
inside the ingest block).

Closes #194

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
